source-discipline.reason: Taxonomy/schema decision; no new source-existence claims are made. Population set adopted from the owner-supplied Access Taxonomy v0.1, whose draft/unvalidated provenance is recorded in the DR.

best-practice-synthesis-routing.reason: No best_practice_synthesis written; this is a governance/schema change to the population code set.

evidence-verification-gate-for-synthesis.reason: The re-key was verified rebuild-reproducible and against the pre-change integrity baseline before adoption: A03 (item_population_links -> populations FK) passes, no new integrity failures introduced, 13 evidence cells preserved, item-link merges deduped deterministically (366->363).

adherence-logging-and-attestation.reason: This attestation logs the DG-NON decision and binds it to doctrine SHA 0f2f525.

bias_direction: Risk of over-eager execution: adopting a self-declared v0.1 unvalidated draft as the operative population scheme could over-privilege internal tidiness over the lived-experience validation the taxonomy itself flags as missing. Mitigated by recording the draft provenance, keeping the set revisable, and deferring the irreversible pieces (prose sweep, parent_code drop) rather than forcing them.
independent_reviewer_counterclaim: A reviewer could argue this change is premature and structurally destructive: it retires a ratified scheme and re-keys the only populated synthesis work against an unvalidated draft, before any disabled-community consultation. The counter-check is that all 13 evidence cells and 363 links were conserved, the change is rebuild-reproducible and revertible on a branch behind a PR, and it was owner-directed with explicit sub-decisions preserving ALL and MH.
